cover_scan: 📱 Scan with your phone and join in
cover_intro: Tap your way through small games and learn how large language models work. At the end, a short quiz gives you personal reflection tips.
cover_sync_note: 👥 Shared session active. Join in and go through the chapters together with the group.
lobby_wait_approved: Hang tight. The host starts the session for everyone together.
lobby_wait_unapproved: You joined a bit later. The host still needs to let you in.
admin_login_sub: Sign in to control the shared session.
adm_lobby_status: Status: lobby. Wait until everyone's here, then start.
adm_nudge: ✅ Most are done. Ready for chapter {n}
adm_unlocked_upto: unlocked up to chapter {n}. Tap a chapter to unlock:
adm_solo_desc: Progress of people outside any journey.
confirm_end_journey: Really end the journey? The app then runs in solo mode again.
confirm_cancel_lobby: Cancel the lobby? The waiting people are removed and the journey is deleted.
confirm_delete_journey: Delete this journey for good? All its data will be lost.
gl_hint: Tip: tap underlined terms for a quick explanation.
predict_correct: ✅ Exactly! The model picks the most likely token. You're thinking like an LLM!
predict_wrong: ✔️ Not wrong! {chosen} fits fine. But {best} appeared more often in this context in the training data, so it's more likely. The model picks the most likely, not the one right answer.
tok_hint: Type text. A real GPT tokenizer splits it into tokens live:
tok_note: A leading “·” means the space belongs to that token — spaces are never separate. Common words are a single token; longer or rarer words break into fragments.
pipe_hint: From prompt to answer, step by step:
ctx_hint: Window size: {n} messages (+ system prompt stays fixed). Send messages until it overflows:
match_hint: Which technique is behind each example?
agent_hint: Put the steps of the agent loop in the right order. Tap to select, tap again to remove (everything after moves up).
agent_ok: ✅ Perfect! Exactly this loop (plan, act, check, repeat) turns an LLM into an agent.
agent_no: Almost! Here's the right order (green = correctly placed). Fix it with "Reset" and try again.
classify_hint: Would you blindly trust the model here? Tap each card to reveal the assessment.
emb_caption: 8 of many numbers. Similar words, similar bars.
emb_correct: ✅ Correct! {w} is closest to {target}. Similar meaning, similar numbers.
emb_close: Close. But compare the distances to {target}. Which word is really closest?
ss_hint: Pick a question and tap the {k} documents that fit best in meaning. The map reveals the answer afterwards.
ss_need_query: Pick a question above first — then you can tap the documents.
ss_result: Hits: {hit} of {k}. The search takes the nearest vectors: {list}.
attn_hint: Tap the highlighted word and see what it "looks at".
plab_v_high: ✅ Strong! Clear, concrete and reliable. The model answers dependably.
plab_v_mid: 🔧 Better already. What's still missing: role, format or an example?
plab_v_low: Still very vague. The model has to guess what you want.
rag_s1_d: The question becomes a meaning vector.
cost_hint: Turn the dials and watch the cost live.
cost_note: 💡 A large input context (e.g. a long system prompt) drives cost — on every call.
inj_harmless: This line is harmless. Which one contains a hidden instruction?
offline_banner: ✈️ Offline. Counting is paused during the session. Turn the connection back on to keep going.
mag_static_h: This is what the text magician looks like (example)
mag_static_intro: No problem. Here's how the text magician works:
mag_hint: Runs on your device, no server. Type an English sentence and transform it:
mag_airplane: ✈️ Tip: go ahead and switch on airplane mode. It still runs, entirely on your device.
mag_load_error: The model couldn't load here (device/browser or network). No worries. Here's what the text magician would look like:
mag_idle: Load a small English language model (~250 MB). Then your device generates real text live, with no server at all.
aic_body: We can load a small English language model (~250 MB) that later runs entirely on your device — no server. It loads quietly in the background while you learn (best on Wi-Fi). Don't need it? That's fine too — you can use the app normally.
quiz_intro_lead: {n} questions. At the end you'll see what stuck, and get personal tips for the reflection round.
quiz_intro_sub: No grade, no stress. It's just about seeing together which topics we should dig into more.
res_sub_perfect: All correct! You've got the basics down. The cards below still work as conversation anchors.
res_sub_weak: Below you'll see which topics are most worth taking into the reflection round.
res_reflection_weak: These topics didn't quite stick yet. Take them into the conversation:
egg_body: At maximum temperature, even the most unlikely gets a chance. Just like this easter egg.
egg_tell_admins: Tell the admins and grab your prize. 🎁
egg_found_hint: → tell the admins & grab your prize
